title: psoralea mephitica var. retrorsa
intro: Psoralea mephitica var. retrorsa, also known as the Indian turnip or locoweed, is a plant species that belongs to the legume family. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Psoralea mephitica var. retrorsa include its ability to improve skin health, reduce stress, and support immune function. Traditionally, the plant is used to prepare teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
